name: linux
description: Comprehensive Linux system commands for Debian/Ubuntu and Fedora/RHEL distributions

system.name: uname
system.description: Print system information (kernel, hostname, architecture)

system.name: hostname
system.description: Show or set the system hostname

options.name: -f
options.description: Display the FQDN (Fully Qualified Domain Name)

options.name: -i
options.description: Display the IP address of the host

options.name: -I
options.description: Display all IP addresses of the host

system.name: hostnamectl
system.description: Control the system hostname and related settings (systemd)

subcommands.name: status
subcommands.description: Show current hostname and system info

subcommands.name: set-icon-name
subcommands.description: Set the icon name for the host

subcommands.name: set-chassis
subcommands.description: Set the chassis type (desktop, laptop, server, vm, container)

system.name: uptime
system.description: Show how long the system has been running, load averages

system.name: dmesg
system.description: Print or control the kernel ring buffer (boot/hardware messages)

options.name: -H
options.description: Human-readable output with colors and timestamps

options.name: -c
options.description: Clear the ring buffer after printing

system.name: lsb_release
system.description: Print distribution-specific information (Debian/Ubuntu)

system.name: date
system.description: Display or set the system date and time

options.name: -d
options.description: Display time for a given date string

system.name: timedatectl
system.description: Control the system clock and timezone settings (systemd)

subcommands.name: status
subcommands.description: Show current time and timezone settings

options.name: --no-pager
options.description: Do not pipe output into a pager

system.name: cal
system.description: Display a calendar

options.description: Show previous, current, and next month

system.name: locale
system.description: Display current locale settings or available locales

system.name: localectl
system.description: Control system locale and keyboard layout settings (systemd)

subcommands.name: status
subcommands.description: Show current locale and keyboard settings

system.name: env
system.description: Print or modify the environment, run a command in modified environment

options.description: End each line with NUL instead of newline

system.name: export
system.description: Set or export environment variables for child processes

options.name: -n
options.description: Remove the export property from a variable

system.name: alias
system.description: Create command shortcuts/aliases

system.name: unalias
system.description: Remove shell aliases

system.name: history
system.description: Display or manipulate the command history list

options.name: -d
options.description: Delete a specific history entry by offset

options.name: -w
options.description: Write current history to history file

options.name: -r
options.description: Read history file and append to current list

options.name: -a
options.description: Append new history lines to history file

examples.description: Repeat last command starting with docker

system.name: echo
system.description: Display a line of text or variable value

system.name: printf
system.description: Format and print data (more control than echo)

system.name: tee
system.description: Read from stdin and write to both stdout and file(s)

options.name: -a
options.description: Append to files instead of overwriting

system.name: xargs
system.description: Build and execute commands from standard input

options.description: Input items are NUL-separated (use with find -print0)

options.name: --no-run-if-empty
options.description: Do not run if stdin is empty

system.name: watch
system.description: Execute a program periodically, showing output fullscreen

examples.description: Monitor disk usage with diff highlighting

system.name: crontab
system.description: Schedule recurring commands/jobs using cron

options.name: -u
options.description: Specify user whose crontab to manage

examples.command: # m h dom mon dow command

system.name: at
system.description: Schedule a one-time command to run at a specific time

options.name: -d
options.description: Delete a job by ID (same as atrm)

system.name: shutdown
system.description: Halt, power off, or reboot the system

system.name: reboot
system.description: Reboot the system

system.name: poweroff
system.description: Power off the system

system.name: clear
system.description: Clear the terminal screen

options.name: -x
options.description: Do not attempt to clear scrollback buffer

system.name: set
system.description: Set or unset shell options and positional parameters

options.name: -o pipefail
options.description: Return error if any pipe command fails

system.name: source
system.description: Execute commands from a file in the current shell (also '.')

system.name: arch
system.description: Print machine architecture (equivalent to uname -m)

system.name: w
system.description: Show who is logged in and what they are doing

examples.description: Show logged-in users and their activity

system.name: who
system.description: Show who is logged in

options.name: -q
options.description: Quick: names and count of logged-in users

system.name: whoami
system.description: Print current effective username

system.name: last
system.description: Show listing of last logged-in users

options.name: -i
options.description: Display IP addresses instead of hostnames

system.name: loginctl
system.description: Control the systemd login manager

subcommands.name: terminate-user
subcommands.description: Terminate all sessions of a user

system.name: sysctl
system.description: Configure kernel parameters at runtime

options.name: --system
options.description: Load settings from all system config files

file.name: touch
file.description: Create empty files or update file timestamps

options.name: -d
options.description: Use specified date instead of current time

file.name: mkdir
file.description: Create directories

options.name: -p
options.description: Create parent directories as needed (no error if exists)

file.name: rmdir
file.description: Remove empty directories

options.name: -p
options.description: Remove parent directories if they become empty

examples.description: Remove c, then b, then a if empty

file.name: ln
file.description: Create hard and symbolic (soft) links

options.name: -n
options.description: Do not dereference target if it is a symlink

file.name: stat
file.description: Display detailed file or filesystem status

options.name: -f
options.description: Display filesystem status instead of file

examples.description: Show filesystem info for root partition

file.name: file
file.description: Determine file type

options.name: -z
options.description: Try to look inside compressed files

examples.description: Check if binary is ELF, script, etc.

file.name: head
file.description: Output the first part of files

options.name: -q
options.description: Never print headers for multiple files

file.name: tail
file.description: Output the last part of files

options.name: -f
options.description: Follow: output appended data as file grows

file.name: wc
file.description: Print newline, word, and byte counts for files

file.name: sort
file.description: Sort lines of text files

options.name: -u
options.description: Unique: output only first of equal lines

options.name: -s
options.description: Stabilize sort (preserve input order of equal keys)

file.name: uniq
file.description: Report or omit repeated lines (input must be sorted)

file.name: cut
file.description: Remove sections from each line of files

file.name: tr
file.description: Translate or delete characters

options.name: -c
options.description: Complement SET1 (use all chars NOT in SET1)

file.name: diff
file.description: Compare files line by line

file.name: patch
file.description: Apply a diff/patch file to originals

file.name: basename
file.description: Strip directory and suffix from filenames

file.name: dirname
file.description: Strip last component from filename (return directory part)

file.name: realpath
file.description: Print the resolved absolute pathname (resolve symlinks)

examples.description: Show absolute path of current directory
Resolve tilde and show full path

file.name: readlink
file.description: Print value of a symbolic link or canonical filename

options.name: -e
options.description: Like -f but all components must exist

options.name: -m
options.description: Like -f but no component needs to exist

file.name: tree
file.description: List directory contents in a tree-like format

file.name: install
file.description: Copy files and set attributes (often used in Makefiles)

file.name: dd
file.description: Convert and copy a file (low-level disk/data copying)

options.name: skip=
options.description: Skip N blocks at input start

options.name: seek=
options.description: Skip N blocks at output start

examples.command: dd if=ubuntu.iso of=/dev/sdb bs=4M status=progress conv=fsync

file.name: split
file.description: Split a file into fixed-size pieces

file.name: mktemp
file.description: Create a temporary file or directory safely

options.name: -d
options.description: Create a directory instead of a file

options.name: -u
options.description: Do not create anything; merely print a name (unsafe)

file.name: shred
file.description: Securely overwrite a file to hide its contents, optionally delete

options.name: -u
options.description: Deallocate and remove file after overwriting

options.name: -z
options.description: Add a final overwrite with zeros to hide shredding

file.name: strings
file.description: Print printable strings from binary files

options.name: -e
options.description: Encoding (s=7-bit, S=8-bit, b=16-bit BE, l=16-bit LE)

file.name: lsof
file.description: List open files and the processes using them

options.name: -i
options.description: List network connections (optionally filter by port)

options.name: -p
options.description: Show files opened by specific PID

options.name: -c
options.description: Show files opened by command name

options.name: +D
options.description: Recursively list open files in directory

file.name: column
file.description: Format text into columns/table layout

file.name: paste
file.description: Merge lines of files side by side

options.name: -s
options.description: Paste one file at a time (serial)

file.name: nl
file.description: Number lines of files

file.name: od
file.description: Dump files in octal/hex and other formats

options.name: -A
options.description: Address radix: d=decimal, o=octal, x=hex, n=none

file.name: xxd
file.description: Make a hexdump or do the reverse

options.description: Reverse: convert hex dump back to binary

file.name: iconv
file.description: Convert text encoding between character sets

examples.description: Windows to UTF-8, skip bad chars

network.name: ip
network.description: Show/manipulate routing, devices, tunnels (modern replacement for ifconfig/route)

examples.command: sudo ip addr add 192.168.1.100/24 dev eth0
sudo ip link set eth0 up
sudo ip route add default via 192.168.1.1

network.name: ifconfig
network.description: Configure or display network interface parameters (legacy, use ip instead)

options.name: -a
options.description: Show all interfaces including inactive ones

network.name: ping
network.description: Send ICMP echo request to test network connectivity

network.name: traceroute
network.description: Print the route packets take to a network host

options.name: -I
options.description: Use ICMP echo instead of UDP

network.name: mtr
network.description: Network diagnostic tool combining traceroute and ping

examples.description: Report with ASN info, no DNS

network.name: nslookup
network.description: Query DNS nameservers interactively or non-interactively

options.name: -type=
options.description: Record type (A, AAAA, MX, NS, TXT, SOA, PTR)

options.name: -t
options.description: Query type (A, AAAA, MX, TXT, NS, SOA)

examples.description: Show all listening TCP/UDP with PIDs

network.name: netstat
network.description: Print network connections, routing tables, statistics (legacy, use ss)

examples.description: Show all listening ports with PIDs

network.name: route
network.description: Show/manipulate the IP routing table (legacy, use ip route)

network.description: Netcat: read/write network connections (TCP/UDP Swiss army knife)

options.name: -z
options.description: Zero I/O mode (scan without sending data)

network.name: whois
network.description: Query WHOIS information for a domain or IP

network.description: NetworkManager command-line tool for managing connections

nmcli connection add type ethernet con-name eth0 ifname eth0

network.description: Query and control network driver and hardware settings

examples.command: sudo ethtool -s eth0 speed 1000 duplex full autoneg off

options.name: -c
options.description: Run as client, connect to host

network.name: socat
network.description: Multipurpose relay for bidirectional data transfer (advanced netcat)

subcommands.name: status
subcommands.description: Show resolver status for all interfaces

options.name: -S
options.description: Search for package owning a file

options.name: -qf
options.description: Query which package owns a file

subcommands.description: Create an alias for a snap command

package.description: Query the APT package cache (search, show, dependencies)

process.description: Send a signal to a process (default: SIGTERM)

process.description: Kill processes by name

options.description: Kill only processes owned by user

options.description: Report if signal was sent successfully

options.description: Match against full command line (not just name)

options.description: List PID and full command line

process.description: Run a command with modified scheduling priority (-20 highest to 19 lowest)

process.description: Alter priority of running processes

process.description: Run a command immune to hangups (survives terminal close)
